title: [Bug]: Incorrectly throwing an exception when only a single message is passed to the OPENAI API model.
body:
**Describe the bug**
The OPENAI API requires the content passed to the model to contain a "role" field. Therefore, there is a section in line 196 of `src/agentscope/models/openai_model.py` that checks whether the incoming message includes the "role" field.
```
 # step2: checking messages
        if not all("role" in msg and "content" in msg for msg in messages):
            raise ValueError(
                "Each message in the 'messages' list must contain a 'role' "
                "and 'content' key for OpenAI API.",
            )
```
However, according to this code, it assumes that the messages passed to the model is a list and checks each dictionary within the list for the presence of the "role" key. But if the messages variable passed to the model is itself just a single dictionary, this logic will also throw an exception of "missing role field". There is no other code to ensure that the type of the messages variable is a list .

**To Reproduce**
1. Define a customer agent:
[agents.py]
```python
class TestCustomerAgent(AgentBase):

    def reply(self, x: dict = None) -> dict:
        if x is not None:
            self.memory.add(x)

        # This is right, because memory.get_memory() will return a list of dict.
        # prompt = self.engine.join(
        #     self.memory.get_memory(),
        # )

        # This will cause an Exception, which should not be happened. The msg contains the role and content keys.
        prompt = Msg(name="user", role="user", content=str(x["content"]))

        response = self.model(prompt).text
        msg = Msg(self.name, response)
        self.speak(msg)

        # Record the message to memory and return it
        self.memory.add(msg)
        return msg
```
2. call this agent with a single message.
[main,py]
```python
test_agent = TestCustomerAgent(
    name="Tester",
    sys_prompt="You are a very helpful assistant.",
    model_config_name="gpt-3.5-turbo",
)
if __name__ == "__main__":
    init_msg = Msg(name="user", role="user", content="prints the current local time")
    x = test_agent(init_msg)
    print(x)
```

The built-in agent of the framework ensures that the type of the incoming message is a list through some functions. However, when developers define their own agents, you cannot guarantee this.

**Error messages**
This is the incorrectly throwing exception:
```
2024-03-15 09:24:03.880 | ERROR    | agentscope.utils.logging_utils:write:41 - Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:\...\main.py", line 138, in <module>
    x = test_agent(init_msg)
  File "C:\...\agentscope\src\agentscope\agents\agent.py", line 117, in __call__
    res = self.reply(*args, **kwargs)
  File "C:\...\agents.py", line 251, in reply
    response = self.model(prompt).text
  File "C:\...\agentscope\src\agentscope\models\model.py", line 159, in checking_wrapper
    return model_call(self, *args, **kwargs)
  File "C:\...\agentscope\src\agentscope\models\openai_model.py", line 197, in __call__
    raise ValueError(
ValueError: Each message in the 'messages' list must contain a 'role' and 'content' key for OpenAI API.
```

**Environment (please complete the following information):**
 - AgentScope Version: 0.0.1 
 - Python Version: 3.10
 - OS: windows

linked_prs.title: [Hot Fix]Optimize error messages of ModelWrapper
linked_prs.patch:
diff --git a/src/agentscope/models/dashscope_model.py b/src/agentscope/models/dashscope_model.py
index bb08b7713e..0727e9906b 100644
--- a/src/agentscope/models/dashscope_model.py
+++ b/src/agentscope/models/dashscope_model.py
@@ -157,7 +157,7 @@ def __call__(
         if not isinstance(messages, list):
             raise ValueError(
                 "Dashscope `messages` field expected type `list`, "
-                f"got `{type(messages)}` instead."
+                f"got `{type(messages)}` instead.",
             )
         if not all("role" in msg and "content" in msg for msg in messages):
             raise ValueError(
diff --git a/src/agentscope/models/openai_model.py b/src/agentscope/models/openai_model.py
index 28c1d18c1f..6b1c962140 100644
--- a/src/agentscope/models/openai_model.py
+++ b/src/agentscope/models/openai_model.py
@@ -175,7 +175,7 @@ def __call__(
         if not isinstance(messages, list):
             raise ValueError(
                 "OpenAI `messages` field expected type `list`, "
-                f"got `{type(messages)}` instead."
+                f"got `{type(messages)}` instead.",
             )
         if not all("role" in msg and "content" in msg for msg in messages):
             raise ValueError(

ai_judgment.response:
Yes.  
This is an agent issue because it involves the integration and invocation of an LLM provider (OpenAI API), specifically handling the message format and parameter validation when calling the model within an agent system. It directly relates to LLM operation and provider compatibility in agent workflows.
